title: The PESO Model® Diagnostic: How HubSpot Turned Owned Media Into a Moat
tldr: Gini Dietrich breaks down how HubSpot's owned-media strategy became a defensible asset even after losing 75% of blog traffic to AI. A concrete case study in surviving content decay via owned channels.
why_fresh: fresh case study on AI-era traffic collapse
angle: What HubSpot's traffic collapse teaches about building owned-media moats when AI eats search referrals.

title: The B2B Blog Is Collapsing. The Companies Replacing It Are Becoming Publishers.
tldr: Argues the traditional B2B blog is dying and companies are shifting to full brand-publisher models. Directly relevant to owned-media and content-strategy shifts.
why_fresh: emerging shift in B2B content models
angle: From blog to newsroom: why B2B brands must think like publishers now.

title: From influencer feeds to pop-up cafes: Tips for expanding comms beyond the screen
tldr: AG1 uses a traveling cafe to build trust and explain products, moving a digital-first brand into physical, human touchpoints. A tangible campaign breakdown on experiential comms.
angle: Why digital-first brands are going physical to rebuild human trust.

title: Backrooms Made $370 Million Because Nobody Owns It. Hollywood's IP Playbook Just Broke
tldr: A crowd-created horror universe generated $370M precisely because it's open and un-owned, upending traditional IP control. A striking case for community-generated content and narrative ownership.
angle: What Backrooms teaches brands about letting audiences own the story.

title: Your Crisis Communications Plan is Solving the Wrong Problem
tldr: Argues modern crisis preparedness needs an integrated communications operating system, not a static plan. Reframes crisis comms for today's always-on environment.
angle: Why your crisis plan is a fossil—and what an always-on comms OS looks like.

title: AI Traffic Conversion Rates: New Research from 97 B2B Websites and 29M visits
tldr: Fresh research on how visitors from AI search engines convert versus traditional search, across 29M visits. Data content strategists can act on immediately.
why_fresh: new original research on AI traffic
angle: What 29M visits reveal about whether AI-search traffic actually converts.

title: As AI automates media work, Omnicom Media is rethinking the agency business model
tldr: Omnicom is restructuring its agency model as AI automates core media tasks. A signal of how AI is reshaping the economics of the media/agency business.
angle: When AI eats the billable hour: what agencies' restructuring means for content teams.

title: The 'renaissance of real': the new rules of marketing in the AI era
tldr: Argues that as AI floods channels with synthetic content, authentic, human, 'real' marketing becomes the differentiator. Directly speaks to human-centered content over polish.
angle: The renaissance of real: why human imperfection is the new premium in an AI-saturated feed.

title: The Fortune 500 Is Deleting the CMO Title
tldr: A wave of Fortune 500 companies are dropping the CMO title, signaling a shift in how marketing leadership and influence are structured. Relevant to executive positioning and comms leadership.
angle: The disappearing CMO: what killing the title says about marketing's shrinking C-suite clout.

title: Future of Marketing Briefing: The case for and against the death of the big brand ad
tldr: Debates mass-reach brand advertising versus targeting the small group of people who already trust you. A useful lens on audience strategy and narrow-vs-broad reach.
angle: A million strangers or a thousand believers: rethinking reach in content strategy.

title: How Transparent Should I Be About AI?
tldr: CJR unpacks how and why writers should disclose AI use—from fact-checking to drafting. Timely governance guidance as AI enters everyone's workflow.
angle: Drawing the AI disclosure line: a practical governance framework for content teams.

title: YouTube on TV: What Marketers Need to Know
tldr: YouTube's aggressive push into living-room TV viewing changes how content should be created and optimized for big screens. A shift in media-consumption habits marketers must adapt to.
angle: YouTube is now a TV channel: rethinking video content for the 65-inch screen.

title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
tldr: Patreon cut ~93 jobs, with the CEO citing AI's transformation of how work gets done. A creator-economy bellwether feeling AI's structural pressure.
angle: What Patreon's AI-era layoffs signal for the creator economy's business model.

title: Кристофер Нолан – как работает самый успешный режиссер современного кино
tldr: Ten principles behind how Christopher Nolan builds intelligent blockbusters. A craft breakdown of storytelling and audience engagement, timed to his 'Odyssey' premiere.
angle: Nolan's storytelling principles applied to business narrative and audience transportation.

title: Unhinged Marketing in 2026: What It Is and How to Make It Work
tldr: Explores the 'unhinged' brand-voice trend on social and how to execute it without wrecking reputation. Ties to human, personality-driven content over corporate polish.
angle: Unhinged but on-brand: where personality-led content pays off and where it backfires.
